stimulus: Several members of the city planning commission have pushed back against proposals to livestream zoning hearings, worried that participants would play to the camera and shift the deliberations toward soundbites. ______blank the commissioners fear that most residents would never watch the full hearings, viewing only brief excerpts that could be edited to distort the discussion.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "Furthermore" logically signals that the claim in this sentence—that commissioners worry viewers would watch only brief, edited excerpts—adds a second, independent concern to the one raised in the previous sentence about participants playing to the camera.

Choice A is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that this sentence exemplifies the previous concern about participants performing for cameras, when in fact it raises a distinct concern about audiences. Choice C is incorrect because "however" illogically signals that this concern contrasts with the previous one, when both concerns point in the same direction. Choice D is incorrect because "by comparison" illogically signals that the two concerns are being compared along a shared measurable dimension, when the sentence simply adds another worry.
